rulings.Case Title: RICHARD CARDOZA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT SQUARE D COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Transferred to Department 301 to be heard on October 1, 2009, per recusal of Judge Woolard. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL A DAVIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant shifts the burden of proof and no triable issues of fact raised. Defendant testified at deposition that not able to identify the product or the specifics of its use, Plaintiff's declaration objectionable per D'Amico. Even if plaintiff's declaration admissible, no evidence FixAll contained asbestos. Plaintiff's exposure in the 1970's, no evidence it contained asbestos at the time of plaintiff's use. Ay declaration not admissible. Lewis deposition not admissible as irrelevant. Dowd and Wellington depositions not admissible under Evidence Code section 240, no showing of unavailability. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED GROWTH, LLC VS. SURVEY AMERICA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2009, line 14, DEFENDANT BROWN CONSULTING ENGINEERS Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Motion granted. Defendant did not purposefully avail itself of the priviledge of conducting activities in California. Defendant was hired by an Indiana company, never traveled to California, did not work in California, and did not expect to be paid in California. The case involves land in Utah, where the witnesses are located. It would not be reasonable to exercise jurisdiction over defendant. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY P. LEIBEL VS. CONSTANCE M. O'BRIEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2009, line 17, DEFENDANT JOHN O'BRIEN, and, CONSTANCE O'BRIEN DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend for plaintiff to dismiss the insurer(s) and delete references to them. Plaintiff may not sue both the insured and the insurer in the same action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY P. LEIBEL VS. CONSTANCE M. O'BRIEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike On Behalf Of Constance O'Brien And John O'Brien;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2009, line 17, DEFENDANT JOHN O'BRIEN, and, CONSTANCE O'BRIEN Motion To Strike. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. Driving with a suspended license does not rise to the level of oppression, fraud, or malice to support punitive damages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA B. MANCE VS. STATE FARM INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2009, line 18, DEFENDANT STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot plead a third party bad faith claim against defendant. Defendant's settlement negotiations and actions do not support a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)
